rationale: Russian strikes killing 11 and destroying a historic Kyiv cathedral represent a fresh qualitative escalation in symbolic and human terms for Ukrainian residents; the US-Iran deal is background noise. Score adjusted down: ongoing situation without qualitative change.

top_events.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
top_events.summary: Fresh deadly Russian air strikes on Kyiv killed 11 people and destroyed a historic cathedral — a qualitatively new symbolic and human escalation directly affecting Ukrainian daily life and morale.

top_events.title: 'Island surrounded by war': Crimeans panic amid Ukrainian attacks
top_events.summary: Reports of panic in Crimea amid Ukrainian strikes signal continued Ukrainian military pressure — relevant context for Ukrainian residents but no new strategic shift.

top_events.title: Iran deal — 'Netanyahu's life project failed with US-Iran deal'
top_events.summary: The US-Iran deal is largely irrelevant to Ukrainian daily life; Western attention and resources remain the key variable, and no confirmed shift in US support for Ukraine is indicated.
